texto: Modern life is, for most of us, a kind of serfdom to mortgage, job and the constant assault to consume. Although we have more time and money than ever before, most of us have little sense of control over our own lives. It is all connected to the apathy that means fewer and fewer people vote. Politicians don’t listen to us anyway. Big business has all the power; religious extremism all the fear. But in the garden or allotment we are king or queen. It is our piece of outdoors that lays a real stake to the planet.
explicacao: Citação de Monty Don sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “Modern life is, for most of us, a kind of serfdom to mortgage, job and the constant assault to co…” articula negocios, allotment, apathy em registro de negocios, com relevância para contextos como apathy e control.
